Pope Francis arrives in Corsica two days before his 88th birthday. Francis’ one-day trip to capital Ajaccio, birthplace of Napoleon, is the first papal visit to the French island and will include a meeting with French President Emmanuel Macron. Read more here: https://bit.ly/3Dhu9Y5

items.title: Palestinians in Syria's Yarmouk camp hope they can return and rebuild their homes
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Following the toppling of the Syrian government residents of the Yarmouk camp outside Damascus, which had housed much of the Palestinian diaspora since 1957, are beginning to return to homes they fled. While much was heavily damaged by government bombardment, many are thinking about rebuilding their houses and coming back for good. (AP Video by Malak Harb) Read more here: https://bit.ly/4fnQWi2

items.title: Moment migrants were rescued by helicopter from a capsized dinghy in Greek waters
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
According to Greek authorities, five people are dead, and 39 people have been rescued while search and rescue operations are ongoing off the coast of Crete. Footage released by the Hellenic Navy shows a migrant being rescued and pulled to safety by a navy helicopter.

items.title: Activists release 'undercover' images of foxes at Finnish fur farms to push for EU ban of trade
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Finnish advocacy group Oikeutta Elaimille and Humane Society International have released images taken from an “undercover investigation” at three fur farms which they say shows the behind-the-scenes realities of the fur trade. But fur farming group FIFUR said the images were "selective", and that the industry has the "highest welfare standards of any form of animal husbandry."

items.title: What happens next in South Korea?
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
Following a parliament vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol, South Korea's opposition leader urged the Constitutional Court to rule swiftly on a bid to remove him from office. But Korea Univeristy Law Professor Chang Young-soo says there is only a "50-50 chance" of Yoon being removed.
